# Sand

> Sand is a strongly-typed Rust framework for generating vanilla Minecraft
> Java Edition datapacks (and optional resource packs). It emits normal
> `data/<namespace>/...` datapack files — functions, tags, advancements,
> predicates, recipes, loot tables, item modifiers, and 1.21+ item
> components — via an attribute-first authoring model (`#[function]`,
> `#[component(Load)]`, `#[component(Tick)]`), typed state, typed
> conditions, typed execute chains, and generated typed command builders.
> Raw commands and raw JSON/SNBT are explicit, deliberate escape hatches,
> not the default authoring path.

Sand is **alpha** software, not published to crates.io — build from
workspace source (`cargo build -p sand --release`). Core attribute-first
authoring (functions, load/tick, typed state/condition/execute/text,
generated commands, scaffold) is stable. Events, dialogs, and resource-pack
generation are alpha; `mcfunction!` and registry coverage for unreleased
Minecraft versions are experimental. Verified Minecraft support spans
1.18.0 through 26.2.0 (`sand-core/src/version.rs`); unknown/future versions
fall back to conservative capabilities rather than erroring. Full detail:
`ai/project-status.yaml`.

## Authority

Source and tests are authoritative over documentation. Where repository
documents disagree, `AGENTS.md` and `ai/` reflect the source-verified,
conservative status — see `ai/known-limitations.md` for specific
documentation contradictions found and how they were resolved. Do not infer
that an API, command, or capability exists from naming conventions; check
the capability manifest or grep source first.

## Key resources

- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md) — operating rules for coding agents: authority
  order, authoring model, task workflow, verification requirements.
- [ai/project-status.yaml](ai/project-status.yaml) — machine-readable
  repo-level status: version, maturity, CLI command status, test/validation
  status, supported Minecraft range.
- [ai/capability-manifest.yaml](ai/capability-manifest.yaml) —
  machine-readable capability inventory (functions, state, events,
  datapack components, systems, CLI, escape hatches) with stable IDs,
  status, and evidence.
- [ai/authoring-guide.md](ai/authoring-guide.md) — decision-oriented guide
  for turning a user request into Sand code, including a vanilla-possible
  decision tree and anti-hallucination rules.
- [ai/known-limitations.md](ai/known-limitations.md) — consolidated vanilla
  limitations, Sand API gaps, version-sensitive behavior, and documentation
  contradictions, each with a stable ID.
- [ai/recipes/](ai/recipes/README.md) — small, compiled, verified patterns:
  basic pack, stateful system (state + cooldown + conditions), custom item,
  event-driven feature, raw interop.
- [book/src/introduction.md](book/src/introduction.md) — the full mdBook
  user guide (tutorials, manual, recipes reference).
- API documentation — generate locally with `cargo doc --workspace
  --all-features --no-deps`; not hosted externally (not on crates.io/docs.rs
  yet).
- [docs/version-support.md](docs/version-support.md) and
  [docs/version-capabilities.md](docs/version-capabilities.md) — Minecraft
  version / pack_format / feature-flag detail.
- [docs/testing.md](docs/testing.md) and
  [docs/vanilla-reload-validation.md](docs/vanilla-reload-validation.md) —
  how Sand's own test suite works, and the opt-in vanilla-server reload
  harness (`scripts/validate-vanilla-reload.sh`) — note that passing
  `cargo test` is not equivalent to vanilla-reload validation.

## Warning

Do not infer unsupported APIs from naming patterns, book prose, or other
frameworks' conventions. If a capability's status in
`ai/capability-manifest.yaml` is `partial`, `raw_only`, `experimental`, or
`unknown`, say so explicitly rather than presenting it as fully supported.
Some `examples/*.rs` files are stale reference text, not compiled or
CI-checked (see `ai/known-limitations.md`, `LIM-DOC-005`) — prefer
`ai/recipes/` for verified code.
